compiled_state.title: As a user, I can approve a designer's Figma revision by providing the exported PNG
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: An approval command validates that the designer's revision PNG exists at the expected path, performs a read-modify-write tag swap (design:awaiting_import → design:approved) preserving any existing tags, and guards against manifest/image desync by clearing approval if wireframe.svg is regenerated.
**Why**: Gates the story lifecycle — implementation cannot proceed until a human designer's revision is explicitly provided and validated, preventing implementation against stale or missing visual intent.
**Scope**:
- IN: Approval command (new script), PNG file existence validation, read-modify-write tag swap preserving existing tags, desync guard (clear design:approved if wireframe.svg is regenerated after approval)
- OUT: Sprint filtering logic (S3), implementation agent consumption (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running the approval command with a story ID validates that designs/{uuid}/figma-revision.png exists and is non-empty — if missing or empty, the command exits with an error message guiding the user
  ← Satisfies: "approval command that validates file exists at path"
- When this story is complete, the approval command reads the story's current tags, removes design:awaiting_import, appends design:approved, and writes the merged tag list — existing tags on the story are preserved during the swap
  ← Satisfies: "replacing tag with design:approved"
- When this story is complete, if design-wireframe regenerates wireframe.svg for a story that has design:approved, the approval tag is cleared and the user is warned that the revision image may no longer match the manifest
  ← Satisfies: "guard against manifest/image desync"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Handle missing/corrupt PNG gracefully with clear error messages
- [Reliability] ticket-edit --tags= performs full replacement — the approval command MUST use read-modify-write, not direct --tags= assignment
- [Maintainability] Consume tag constants from the same source as S1 and S3

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
